filter_by_text.label: filter by text
filter_by_text.summary: Learn how to keep only rows whose text matches a word, phrase, or pattern by using WHERE with text values and LIKE.

sketch1.title: Using LIKE to match part of a word

ci_stationery_pen.title: Filter by category and name text

sc-text-quotes.title: Text values need quotes
sc-text-quotes.prompt: Which WHERE condition correctly filters rows where the category is Art?
sc-text-quotes.hint: Text values in SQL are written inside single quotes.

help.concept: When you compare a text column in SQL, the text value must be written as a string literal using single quotes.
help.hint_1: Look for the option that compares `category` to a quoted text value.
help.hint_2: The correct condition uses `=` and puts Art inside single quotes.

sc-like-contains.title: Pattern for contains
sc-like-contains.prompt: Which pattern finds product names that contain the text `book` anywhere in the name?
sc-like-contains.hint: Use `%` before and after the text when it can appear anywhere.

help.concept: In a LIKE pattern, `%` stands for any number of characters. Putting `%` on both sides means the text can appear anywhere inside the value.
help.hint_1: A contains search is different from starts with or ends with.
help.hint_2: Choose the pattern with `book` in the middle and `%` on both sides.

mc-like-matches.title: Rows matched by a LIKE filter
mc-like-matches.prompt: Choose all product names from this dataset that would match `WHERE name LIKE '%book%'`.
mc-like-matches.hint: The pattern matches names containing the letters `book` anywhere.

help.concept: A LIKE pattern with `%book%` matches any text value that includes the substring `book`, even if other letters appear before or after it.
help.hint_1: Check each product name for the exact letter sequence `book`.
help.hint_2: Two names in the dataset include `book` as part of the product name.

mc-text-filters-valid.title: Valid text filter conditions
mc-text-filters-valid.prompt: Choose all WHERE conditions that correctly filter text in this lesson.
mc-text-filters-valid.hint: Look for correct use of quotes and valid text operators.

help.concept: Text filtering in this topic uses exact comparison with `=` and pattern matching with `LIKE`, both with quoted text values.
help.hint_1: A valid condition should compare a real column like `name` or `category` to quoted text.
help.hint_2: Two options are valid: one exact match and one pattern match.

dr-query-order-like.title: Build a text filter query
dr-query-order-like.prompt: Arrange the SQL parts to make a query that shows product names containing `Pen`.
dr-query-order-like.hint: A basic query goes SELECT, FROM, then WHERE.

help.concept: A SELECT query follows a standard clause order: choose columns, name the table, then add the filter condition.
help.hint_1: Start with the clause that says what to display.
help.hint_2: The filter using `LIKE '%Pen%'` belongs after the table name.

dr-query-order-exact.title: Order an exact text match query
dr-query-order-exact.prompt: Arrange the SQL parts to return all columns for products in the Stationery category.
dr-query-order-exact.hint: Put the filter after the table clause.

help.concept: Exact text matching uses `WHERE column = 'text'`, and that condition comes after `FROM` in a simple SELECT query.
help.hint_1: The query begins by selecting columns from the table.
help.hint_2: Use the condition with `category = 'Stationery'` as the final clause.

fb-like-keyword.title: Choose the text-matching keyword
fb-like-keyword.prompt: Complete the query so it matches names by pattern.
fb-like-keyword.hint: This keyword is used with `%` wildcards.

help.concept: SQL uses `the missing term` for pattern matching, especially when `%` wildcards are part of the comparison.
help.hint_1: This is not the exact-match operator.
help.hint_2: Choose the keyword used in `name ... '%Pen%'`.

fb-like-keyword.template: SELECT name FROM products WHERE name [blank1] '%Pen%';

fb-text-literal.title: Fill the category text
fb-text-literal.prompt: Complete the filter so it returns only Home products.
fb-text-literal.hint: Read the sentence around the blank and decide what role the missing word plays.

help.concept: The blank should be filled with the term that makes the statement accurate.
help.hint_1: Look at the words before and after the blank to infer what kind of term is needed.
help.hint_2: Choose the option that completes the statement with the clearest meaning.

fb-text-literal.template: SELECT name FROM products WHERE category = [blank1];

ci-art-products.title: Show Art product names
ci-art-products.prompt: Write a query that returns the `name` and `category` columns for products whose category is `Art`. Write the query in the SQL editor, click Run query, and check the results table.
ci-art-products.hint: Use the query pattern from the lesson.

help.concept: This SQL exercise checks whether your query returns the requested result.
help.hint_1: Check the table name and selected columns in the SQL editor.
help.hint_2: Run the query and compare the results table.

ci-name-contains-book.title: Find names containing book
ci-name-contains-book.prompt: Write a query that returns the `name` column for products whose name contains the text `book`. Use a text pattern filter, then click Run query and inspect the results table.
ci-name-contains-book.hint: Use the query pattern from the lesson.

help.concept: This SQL exercise checks whether your query returns the requested result.
help.hint_1: Check the table name and selected columns in the SQL editor.
help.hint_2: Run the query and compare the results table.

ci-stationery-pen.prompt: Write a query that returns the `name` and `price` columns for products that are in the `Stationery` category and whose name contains `Pen`. Write the query in the SQL editor, click Run query, and check the results table.
ci-stationery-pen.hint: Use the query pattern from the lesson.

text-equality.title: Filtering exact text values
text-equality.bodyMarkdown:
Text filters let you keep rows where a text column matches a specific value. In SQL, text values go inside single quotes.

For example, this query returns only products in the `Art` category:

```sql
SELECT name, category
FROM products
WHERE category = 'Art';
```

Step by step:
- `SELECT name, category` chooses the columns to show.
- `FROM products` says which table to read.
- `WHERE category = 'Art'` keeps only rows whose `category` text is exactly `Art`.

With this dataset, the results include `Sketchbook` and `Acrylic Paint Set`.

A common mistake is forgetting the quotes around text. `Art` is text, so write `'Art'`, not `Art`.

like-patterns.title: Using LIKE to match part of a word
like-patterns.bodyMarkdown:
Sometimes you do not want an exact text match. You want rows where text contains certain letters. Use `LIKE` with `%` as a wildcard.

Example:

```sql
SELECT name
FROM products
WHERE name LIKE '%Pen%';
```

How it works:
- `LIKE` compares text by pattern instead of exact equality.
- `%Pen%` means: any text before `Pen`, and any text after `Pen`.
- So this finds names that contain `Pen` anywhere inside the text.

In this dataset, `Fountain Pen` matches that pattern.

You can also place `%` in different positions:
- `'Note%'` = starts with `Note`
- `'%Lamp'` = ends with `Lamp`
- `'%book%'` = contains `book`

combining-text-filters.title: Combining text conditions
combining-text-filters.bodyMarkdown:
You can combine text filters with `AND`, `OR`, and `NOT`.

Worked example:

```sql
SELECT name, category
FROM products
WHERE category = 'Stationery'
  AND name LIKE '%Pen%';
```

Line by line:
- `SELECT name, category` shows the product name and category.
- `FROM products` reads from the products table.
- `WHERE category = 'Stationery'` keeps only stationery items.
- `AND name LIKE '%Pen%'` narrows that set to names containing `Pen`.

In this dataset, that returns `Fountain Pen`.

You can also use `OR` when either condition is allowed:

```sql
SELECT name, category
FROM products
WHERE category = 'Art'
   OR category = 'Home';
```

That returns products from either category.
